mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to de-risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 0.2pp increase in 3 months
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates recovering manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, triggering short-term momentum buying that yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to generate positive 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or signal decays within 2 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins, forcing equity sell-offs and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of high CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS produces positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered cases, or interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth signals an economic slowdown that reduces aggregate demand and corporate profit forecasts, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth crosses below 1.5% quarterly threshold
failure_condition: GDP growth below the threshold fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or market returns remain positive in >50% of deceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and payroll employment indicates synchronized supply-side capacity building and labor absorption, reinforcing economic momentum and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising INDPRO and PAYEMS yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns in >55% of observations, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.
